---
output:
  github_document:
    md_extensions: -smart
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Edit the .Rmd, then run
     knitr::knit("README.Rmd") (no API key needed; there are no live chunks). -->

# closecity <img src="man/figures/logo.png" align="right" height="120" alt="" />

This is the R software development kit for the Close.City API. It returns travel
times from every US census block to nearby places, on foot, by bike, and by public
transit. The data behind [close.city](https://close.city) is served over the
[Close API](https://docs.close.city).

## Install

```r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("henryspatialanalysis/closecity-r")
```

## Documentation

New to the package? Start with the guide, then work through a tutorial.

- [Get started](https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/articles/closecity.html):
  key terms, building a client, making a call and mapping it, output modes, and
  error handling.
- Tutorials
  - [Your first walkability map](https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/articles/first-map.html)
  - [Looking for a home](https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/articles/home-search.html)
  - [The amenity basket](https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/articles/amenity-basket.html)
  - [Competitor walksheds](https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/articles/competitor-walksheds.html)
  - [Efficient token use](https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/articles/token-use.html)
- [Function reference](https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/reference/index.html):
  every client method and helper.

## Reference

- API docs and guides: <https://docs.close.city>
- Interactive API: <https://api.close.city/docs>
- Machine-readable contract: <https://api.close.city/openapi.json>
